title: Who will be driving the buses next week?

meanings.usage: 指工人集体停止工作以要求改善条件
表示敲打或攻击的动作
busman的复数形式，指公共汽车司机
表示主动、无偿地提供服务
指自愿提供服务的人
指在某一领域有专业技能的人
形容在某方面很有经验
指痛苦、压力或困难的缓解
表示焦虑或担忧消除后的感觉
对他人帮助或善意的感激之情
指事物的状态或前提要求
指物理压力或心理压力
表示不同意或抗议
指具体的东西或目的
指某事涉及的范围或大小
指涉及大多数人或事物的
指双方达成的共识或合约
指向他人提供帮助或物品
指乘坐交通工具的旅程
表示骑马、骑车或乘车
表示通过考试、地点或时间

examples.en: The workers have decided to go on strike next week.
examples.zh: 工人们决定下周举行罢工。

examples.en: The meeting is due to begin at 9 o'clock.
To some extent, I agree with you.
examples.zh: 在某种程度上，我同意你的看法。

examples.en: The exam will be held in two days' time.
It was cold; even so, we went swimming.
examples.zh: 天气很冷，尽管如此我们还是去游泳了。

examples.en: So far, everything has been smooth.
examples.zh: 到目前为止，一切都很顺利。

examples.en: The government has stated that the policy will not change.
examples.zh: 政府已经声明政策不会改变。

examples.en: I want to express my gratitude to all my teachers.
examples.zh: 我想对所有老师表达我的感激之情。

examples.en: Many people object that young drivers drive too fast.
examples.zh: 许多人反对年轻人开车太快。

examples.en: The company offered free rides to all passengers during the strike.
examples.zh: 罢工期间公司向所有乘客提供免费乘车服务。

grammar.title: 将来进行时 (Future Continuous Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示将来某一时刻或时间段正在进行的动作
grammar.structure: 主语 + will be + 动词-ing
grammar.usage: 用于描述在将来某个具体时间正在发生的动作，或询问他人的计划，语气比 will + 动词原形 更委婉

examples.en: Who will be driving the buses next week?
examples.zh: 下周谁将驾驶公共汽车？

examples.en: I will be waiting for you at the station at 8 o'clock tomorrow.
examples.zh: 明天八点我会在车站等你。

examples.en: Will you be working late tonight?
examples.zh: 今晚你会工作到很晚吗？

grammar.title: 现在完成时 (Present Perfect Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示过去发生的动作对现在造成的影响或结果
grammar.structure: 主语 + have/has + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于描述过去发生但与现在有联系的事件，常与already, yet, just, so far等连用

examples.en: Busmen have decided to go on strike next week.
examples.zh: 公交车司机已决定下周举行罢工。

examples.en: The students are going to take the test in two days' time. They have not taken it yet.
examples.zh: 学生们将在两天后参加考试，他们还没参加。

grammar.title: be going to 表示将来
grammar.definition: 表示已经计划或决定要做的事，或根据现有迹象判断将要发生的事
grammar.structure: 主语 + am/is/are + going to + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 表示人的主观意图、计划或打算，或根据当前情况预测将要发生的事

examples.en: Many owners of private cars are going to offer 'free rides' to people.
examples.zh: 许多私家车主打算向人们提供免费乘车服务。

examples.en: The students are going to take the test in two days' time.
examples.zh: 学生们将在两天后参加考试。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + have/has decided to + 动词原形

original.en: Busmen have decided to go on strike next week.
original.zh: 公交车司机决定下周举行罢工。

imitations.en: We have decided to travel abroad this summer.
imitations.zh: 我们决定今年夏天去国外旅行。

imitations.en: She has decided to quit her job.

original.en: Who will be driving the buses next week?
original.zh: 下周谁将驾驶公共汽车？

imitations.en: Who will be teaching us English next semester?
Who will be waiting for us at the airport?

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + is/are going to + 动词原形

original.en: The students are going to take the test in two days' time.
original.zh: 学生们将在两天后参加考试。

imitations.en: I am going to visit my grandparents this weekend.
imitations.zh: 我这个周末要去看望祖父母。

imitations.en: They are going to open a new restaurant next month.
imitations.zh: 他们下个月要开一家新餐厅。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + have/has stated that + 从句

original.en: The busmen have stated that the strike will continue.
original.zh: 公交车司机已声明罢工将继续进行。

imitations.en: The manager has stated that the company will not lay off any employees.
imitations.zh: 经理已声明公司不会裁员。

imitations.en: The doctor has stated that the patient needs more rest.
imitations.zh: 医生已声明病人需要更多休息。
